posBrasa.title: POS Brasa — Restaurant Management System
posBrasa.lede: A complete management system for restaurants, churrascarias and cafés, designed and built in-house by Madeira Originals. Built for the real pace of service: fast, reliable, and shaped around how a Madeiran house actually works.
posBrasa.about: We developed POS Brasa from the ground up to meet the daily needs of a live restaurant operation. It's not a generic product — it's a tool shaped at the table, in the kitchen and at the counter, refined every day in real service.
capabilities: Real-time table and order management, synced across waitstaff, kitchen, grill and bar
Automatic thermal printing for kitchen, bar, grill tickets and bills (80mm / 58mm)
Cash and payments — cash, card, MB WAY and split payments
Audited bill corrections with full change history
Menu management with categories, options, VIP pricing and automatic translation
Integrated multilingual website (PT / EN / FR) with an always-updated public menu
Online store with checkout integration and made-to-order production
Robust offline mode — keeps working even when the internet drops
Desktop application (Windows) for POS terminals, with native printing and cash drawer
Management dashboard with sales, dish popularity, expenses and daily reports
Reservations, expenses, inventory and suppliers in one place
posBrasa.disclaimer: Internal-use system. POS Brasa is a solution developed for in-house use in our own restaurants and cafés. It is not certified by the Portuguese Tax Authority (AT) and therefore does not replace a certified invoicing program for issuing official invoices. All documents produced by the system are considered internal management documents.

faqs.a: Yes. Every order leaves our studio in São Vicente, Madeira, and ships worldwide with tracked delivery to Europe, the UK, the US, and most of the rest of the world. Shipping options and live rates are calculated at checkout based on your destination.

faqs.q: Can I order Madeira souvenirs from outside Portugal?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Most of our customers order from outside Portugal — including the Madeiran diaspora in the UK, Channel Islands, South Africa, Venezuela and beyond, plus travellers who discovered us in Funchal or São Vicente and want to order more once they get home.

Yes — fully. Madeira Originals is designed, printed and finished in-house in São Vicente, on the north coast of Madeira. No outsourced fulfilment, no third-party print farms: every piece is pulled, pressed and packed by our own team on the island before it ships to you.

manifesto.body: Madeira Originals was created in São Vicente to bring the island's volcanic, rugged, Atlantic identity into modern apparel. We take the landscapes, the weather, the textures and the culture — and translate them into premium, considered pieces built to live anywhere.

pillars.title: Atlantic Heritage
pillars.body: Rooted in São Vicente — volcanic coastlines, foggy peaks, levadas, and a working island culture.

pillars.title: Editorial Design
pillars.body: Restrained typography, heavyweight construction, minimalist graphics, premium execution.

pillars.title: Made in-house
pillars.body: Designed, printed and finished in our own studio in São Vicente — no third-party print farms.

noTouristTrap.body: We love Madeira too much to reduce it to clichés. Madeira Originals is for people who want something more considered: modern design, local meaning, premium execution, and real cultural reference.

origin.p1: São Vicente sits on the rugged north of Madeira, where the Atlantic meets black basalt cliffs and fog rolls down the laurel forest into the village. It is quiet, wind-shaped, and stubbornly itself.
origin.p2: Madeira Originals exists because this island deserves a brand that translates it properly — not into souvenirs, but into pieces that could live anywhere in the world while staying unmistakably from here.
origin.p3: Atlantic identity. Volcanic landscapes. Contemporary heritage. The brand was built around those three coordinates and refuses to move from them.
origin.imageAlt: São Vicente village under fog on the north coast of Madeira

pillars.title: Authenticity
pillars.body: References that mean something — language, geography, weather, craft. Nothing borrowed from a postcard.

pillars.title: Locality
pillars.body: Designed, printed and finished in our own studio in São Vicente. No outsourced print farms, no third-party fulfilment.

pillars.title: Wearable Design
pillars.body: Pieces that work in Funchal, Lisbon, London or Jersey. Island-rooted, globally cut.

pillars.title: Premium Quality
pillars.body: Heavyweight cotton, considered construction, finishes built to soften with wear rather than fall apart.

pillars.title: 0% Tourist Trap
pillars.body: No magnets. No rooster prints. No lazy graphics. The island deserves better and so do you.

field.p1: The weather changes every hour. ER101 winds along the cliffs above villages built into the rock. Laurel forest holds the moisture in. Salt sticks to everything that faces the sea.
field.p2: Those are the materials of the brand — not as metaphor, but as actual reference. Textures, palettes, weight, restraint.

aboutPage.newsletterBody: First access to new drops, production updates, and stories from Madeira.
aboutPage.lead: Premium streetwear and custom apparel born in São Vicente, Madeira.
aboutPage.heroAlt: Foggy basalt cliffs of São Vicente, Madeira
aboutPage.saoVicenteAlt: Atlantic cliffs of São Vicente, Madeira

contactPage.heading: Let's talk.
contactPage.lead: Whether you're after a piece from the collection or a collaboration — we're based in São Vicente, Madeira and reachable anywhere.

culturePage.heroAlt: Madeira Island volcanic coastline at golden hour
culturePage.lead: Madeira is one of the most distinctive places in the Atlantic — volcanic, maritime, deeply Portuguese, and yet entirely its own. This is the culture that inspires every Madeira Originals design.

cards.title: Levadas
cards.body: Centuries-old irrigation channels carved into the cliffs. Today they double as the island's most loved hiking network — a slow walk through laurisilva forest with the sound of running water.

cards.title: Poncha
cards.body: Aguardente de cana, honey, and citrus, mixed in a glass with a wooden mexelote. Order one in any village bar and you've ordered the island's whole social ritual.

cards.title: Bordado da Madeira
cards.body: A centuries-old embroidery tradition, still produced on the island. The motifs — flora, geometry, restrained palettes — quietly inform a lot of what we design.

longform.h: A volcanic island in the middle of the Atlantic
p: Madeira sits roughly 1,000 km from mainland Portugal and 500 km from the African coast. The island rose from underwater volcanoes and the landscape still shows it — sheer cliffs, cloud-covered peaks, terraced hillsides cut into the slopes, and deep ravines that channel water from the mountains down to the sea.
That geography shapes everything. How Madeirans farm. How they build. How they eat. How they move around an island where the next valley over can be an hour's drive straight down and back up again.

Madeira's culture is fundamentally Atlantic. The food is heavier on the sea than on the olive grove. The light is sharper. The weather changes within the same hour. Madeira shares more in common, in some ways, with the Azores and the Canaries than with Lisbon — and yet remains unmistakably Portuguese.

culturePage.traditionsHeading: Traditions that still live in everyday life

traditions.label: Mercado dos Lavradores
traditions.body: Funchal's working market, where flowers, fruit, and Atlantic fish still arrive daily.

traditions.label: The Madeira toboggan
traditions.body: the wicker sleds from Monte that locals still half-jokingly call the original Madeira "Uber".

traditions.label: Poncha
traditions.body: the island's traditional drink: aguardente de cana, honey, and citrus, mixed in a glass and shared.

traditions.label: Festa da Flor & Festa do Vinho
traditions.body: the flower and wine festivals that still mark the island's calendar.

traditions.label: Bordado da Madeira
traditions.body: Madeira embroidery, a centuries-old craft still produced on the island.

culturePage.diasporaBody: Madeirans have emigrated for generations — South Africa, Venezuela, the Channel Islands, Canada, Australia, the US, the UK. The diaspora keeps Madeira culture alive far beyond the island, and is increasingly driving demand for modern Madeiran products that feel rooted but not nostalgic.
culturePage.inspireBody1: Madeira gives a designer an unusually rich vocabulary: the green-black of volcanic rock, the colour palette of the market, the geometry of terraced agriculture, the typography of old Funchal signage, the patterns of traditional embroidery. It is a small island with an outsized visual identity.
culturePage.inspireBody2: Madeira Originals was built around that. Modern apparel, lifestyle products and accessories — premium materials, contemporary cuts, references that mean something to anyone who knows the island and quietly invite the question from anyone who does not.

souvenirs.guideHeading: What to buy in Madeira (and what to skip).

guide.title: Skip the airport.
guide.body: Funchal airport souvenirs are designed for the 20-minute browse before boarding — not for keeping. Pieces made on the island, by people from the island, hold up better.

guide.title: Look for the demonym.
guide.body: ‘Madeira’ on a magnet is geography. ‘Madeiran’ in the design — language, references, place names — is culture. The second one ages well.

guide.title: Choose pieces you'd wear at home.
guide.body: The best Madeira souvenir is one you'd buy if you weren't on holiday. Premium tees, hoodies, caps, totes — same standard as your everyday wardrobe.

guide.title: Buy direct, ship anywhere.
guide.body: You don't need to fit it in your suitcase. We ship Madeira gifts worldwide from the island — to mainland Portugal, the EU, the UK, the US and beyond.

souvenirs.stockBody: From São Vicente, Madeira to anywhere in the world.
souvenirs.emptyBody: New drops land regularly. Browse the full shop in the meantime.

faqs.q: What are the best souvenirs from Madeira?
faqs.a: The best souvenirs from Madeira are pieces actually made on the island — premium streetwear (Madeira t-shirts, hoodies, caps), poncha and Madeira wine from local producers, embroidery from established Funchal ateliers, and bolo do caco from a real bakery. Skip the airport magnets and rooster figurines.

faqs.q: What to buy in Madeira (and what to skip)?
faqs.a: Buy: Madeiran streetwear and accessories from local brands, Madeira wine from a producer cellar (Blandy's, D'Oliveiras), poncha from a São Vicente or Câmara de Lobos bar, and embroidery from a credentialed atelier. Skip: anything sold in three different airport shops at three different prices, plastic figurines of folkloric dancers, and 'I love Madeira' t-shirts.

In Funchal specifically, look for: streetwear from Madeiran brands (skip the harbour-front tourist shops), Madeira wine from a tasting cellar in São Pedro or Avenida Arriaga, embroidery from Bordal or Patrício & Gouveia on Rua dos Ferreiros, and poncha kits from a proper grocer. Funchal souvenirs done right are island-made, not import-and-print.

faqs.q: Can I order Madeira souvenirs online from outside Portugal?
faqs.a: Yes. Madeira Originals ships premium Madeira gifts and apparel worldwide — to mainland Portugal, the EU, UK, US and the diaspora. You don't need to be on the island, or to fit anything in a suitcase, to buy properly.

faqs.q: What is a typical Madeira gift?
faqs.a: Typical Madeira gifts include Madeira wine, poncha, embroidery (Bordado da Madeira), wickerwork from Camacha, and more recently, modern Madeiran streetwear and accessories. The new wave of Madeira gifts skips the souvenir-shop clichés in favour of pieces locals would actually wear or use.

souvenirs.closingBody: Designed in São Vicente, Madeira. Shipped worldwide.

shipping.title: Shipping
shipping.body:
Pieces are made and finished in our São Vicente studio, then shipped worldwide.

Portugal & Madeira: 2–4 business days.
Europe: 4–7 business days.
UK, US & rest of world: 7–14 business days.

Free shipping in the EU over €60.

returns.title: Returns
returns.body: 14 days to return unworn pieces in original condition for a refund or exchange. Made-to-order and personalised items are non-returnable.

care.title: Care
care.body: Wash cold, inside out. Hang to dry. Do not iron directly on the print. Our heavyweight cotton softens with wear — it is built for the long haul.

shippingPage.seoDescription: Where we ship, how long it takes, and what it costs. Premium garments shipped worldwide from São Vicente, Madeira.
shippingPage.title: From São Vicente to your door.
shippingPage.lead: Every piece is made and finished in our studio on the north coast of Madeira, then sent into the world with care. Here is what to expect.

sections.heading: 01 — Where we ship
sections.body: We ship worldwide. Portugal and Madeira, the European Union, United Kingdom, United States, and most of the rest of the world.

sections.heading: 02 — Processing time
sections.body: Most in-stock pieces leave the studio within 1–2 business days. Made-to-order and personalised pieces take 3–7 business days to print, finish and pack.

sections.heading: 03 — Delivery windows
sections.body:
Portugal & Madeira: 2–4 business days.
European Union: 4–7 business days.
United Kingdom, United States, rest of world: 7–14 business days.

Exact delivery time depends on your local carrier.

sections.heading: 04 — Shipping cost
sections.body:
Flat rate, calculated at checkout based on destination.

Free shipping on EU orders over €60.

sections.heading: 05 — Duties & taxes
sections.body: Orders outside the European Union may be subject to import duties and local taxes on arrival. These are the responsibility of the recipient.

sections.heading: 06 — Tracking
sections.body: Every order ships with tracking. You will receive an email with your tracking number as soon as your parcel leaves the studio.

returnsPage.seoDescription: Our return and exchange policy. 14 days to return unworn pieces in original condition.
returnsPage.title: Honest, simple, no fine print.
returnsPage.lead: We build pieces to be worn for years. If something is not right, we will make it right.

sections.heading: 01 — Window
sections.body: You have 14 days from the day your order arrives to request a return or exchange.

sections.heading: 02 — Condition
sections.body: Pieces must be unworn, unwashed, and in original condition with all tags attached. Returned items that do not meet these conditions may be refused.

sections.heading: 03 — How to start a return
sections.body: Email the studio with your order number and a short note about what you want to return or exchange. We will reply with instructions within 1–2 business days.

sections.heading: 04 — Refunds
sections.body: Once we receive and inspect the return, your refund is issued to the original payment method within 5–10 business days. Original shipping costs are non-refundable.

sections.heading: 05 — Exchanges
sections.body: Need a different size or colour? Tell us in your return email and we will hold the new piece for you while your return is on the way.

sections.heading: 06 — Not eligible
sections.body: Made-to-order, personalised, and final-sale pieces cannot be returned or exchanged. This is always noted clearly on the product page.

carePage.seoDescription: How to care for heavyweight cotton, DTF prints, and embroidered pieces so they last for years.
carePage.title: Built to soften with wear.
carePage.lead: Our pieces are made from heavyweight cotton with premium DTF printing. Treated well, they age beautifully — softer, more personal, more yours.

sections.heading: 01 — Washing
sections.body: Wash cold (max 30°C), inside out, with similar colours. Use a mild detergent. Skip the fabric softener — it coats the cotton and dulls the print over time.

sections.heading: 02 — Drying
sections.body: Hang to dry in the shade. Avoid the tumble dryer when possible — heat is the enemy of both cotton and print. If you must tumble dry, use the lowest setting.

sections.heading: 03 — Ironing
sections.body: Iron inside out, on medium heat. Never iron directly on the print. For embroidered pieces, iron around the embroidery or place a cloth between the iron and the design.

sections.heading: 04 — Storage
sections.body: Fold heavyweight pieces flat rather than hanging — long-term hanging stretches the shoulders. Keep away from direct sunlight to preserve the colour.

sections.heading: 05 — Over time
sections.body: Heavyweight cotton softens with every wash. A small amount of fading and natural texture is part of the material — not a defect. The pieces are built to be worn, often.

sizeGuidePage.seoDescription: Find your fit. Measurements and fit notes for Madeira Originals heavyweight tees, hoodies and accessories.
sizeGuidePage.title: Find your fit.
sizeGuidePage.lead: Our pieces follow a modern unisex cut — relaxed, slightly boxy, true-to-size. Size up for a more oversized streetwear silhouette. All measurements are flat, in centimetres, and may vary by ±1 cm.

sections.heading: How to measure
sections.body: Lay a similar garment flat. Half-chest is measured from armpit to armpit. Length is from the highest point of the shoulder straight down to the hem. Compare to the table below.

sections.heading: Fit notes
sections.body: Heavyweight cotton has a structured drape that softens with wear. If you're between sizes, size down for a clean fit, size up for an oversized streetwear look. Caps are one-size adjustable; totes are one-size.

sections.heading: Still unsure?
sections.body: Email hello@madeiraoriginals.pt with your usual size and how you like it to fit — we'll recommend the right one.

firstDrop.body: A small, curated launch — heavyweight tees, a hoodie, a cap and a tote. Designed in São Vicente, Madeira. Built to last.
firstDrop.soonBody: The First Drop is being prepared in São Vicente. Join the list below to get early access when it lands.

productSpecs.storyFallback: A São Vicente original. Designed on the north coast of Madeira, where the Atlantic meets black basalt and fog rolls down the laurel forest into the village. Built to translate the island — not to advertise it.
productSpecs.fitFallback: Modern unisex fit — relaxed, slightly boxy, true-to-size
productSpecs.printFallback: Premium DTF print, made to survive the wash
productSpecs.sizeIntro: Modern relaxed cut. Size up for a more oversized streetwear silhouette. Measurements are approximate and may vary by ±1 cm.
productSpecs.sizeNote: Half-chest is measured flat from armpit to armpit. Length is from highest point of the shoulder to hem.

custom.seoDescription: Custom T-shirts, staff uniforms, event apparel and branded merch — designed and printed in São Vicente, Madeira. Premium DTF, UV DTF stickers, small-batch friendly.
custom.overline: Custom Apparel · Made in São Vicente
custom.subcopy: Custom T-shirts, staff uniforms, event apparel, and small-batch merch — produced in São Vicente with the same premium materials and finishing as our retail line. Built for restaurants, hotels, cafés, bars, events, clubs and local businesses.
custom.designedIn: Designed in São Vicente, Madeira · Inspired by the Atlantic

services.heading: Premium custom apparel, without the agency overhead.

items.title: Custom T-Shirts
items.body: Heavyweight cotton tees with your design, printed in DTF for long-term wear and crisp colour.

items.title: Staff Uniforms
items.body: Hard-wearing front-of-house and back-of-house pieces. Built for daily service and frequent washing.

items.title: Event Apparel
items.body: Small or large runs for festivals, races, openings and pop-ups. Fast turnaround when you need it.

items.title: Small-Business Merch
items.body: Caps, totes, hoodies and accessories. Treated with the same care as our retail line — never souvenir-grade.

items.title: DTF Printing
items.body: Direct-to-film printing for vivid colour, fine detail and excellent wash durability on cotton blends.

items.title: UV DTF Stickers
items.body: Waterproof, weatherproof die-cut stickers for windows, equipment, packaging and merch.

form.heading: Tell us about your project.
form.sub: Give us a quick brief — we reply within one working day with pricing and next steps.
form.name: Your name *
form.messageHint: Garment type, sizes, deadline, logo / artwork notes, anything else we should know.
form.doneBody: We'll reply within one working day with pricing and next steps.
